meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer's Guide: Master Git with AI for Rapid SaaS Builds
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no SaaS knowledge and professional shipping. Use this intermediate checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code for speed.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the lack of SaaS architecture knowledge requires shifting from manual coding to AI-orchestrated workflows. By integrating Git version control with Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative logic implementation, you transform from a solo freelancer into an automated, high-velocity development machine capable of shipping production-ready SaaS applications.
content.intro:
The screen flickers with a blank terminal, and the weight of a complex SaaS architecture feels like a wall you cannot climb. You have the design vision, but the bridge between 'idea' and 'shippable product' is obstructed by the daunting complexity of Git branching, CI/CD pipelines, and scalable database schemas. You are burning hours debugging boilerplate instead of solving business problems for your clients.

Relief is here. We are replacing manual trial-and-error with a deterministic AI-first workflow. By combining the structural precision of Antigravity with the high-context reasoning of Claude Code, you will automate your Git lifecycle, turning complex feature requests into clean, committed, and deployed code—all while maintaining the professional velocity your clients demand.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a robust project structure that follows enterprise-grade SaaS standards. This prevents technical debt before the first line of code is written.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular, production-ready SaaS folder structure including auth, API, db, and client directories. Ensure clean separation of concerns for a Next.js 14 stack.

steps.title: Automated Version Control Initialization
steps.description: Initialize Git with a standard branching strategy (Gitflow) to ensure your client work is always recoverable and organized.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .gitignore file optimized for Node.js and Next.js, and configure a git branch strategy for feature-based development.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude run 'configure gitflow branches for feature/fix/release'

steps.title: AI-Driven Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map business requirements to code, ensuring the AI writes tests alongside the feature logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a user authentication flow using Supabase. Write unit tests for the login function and include error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement login with Supabase and write vitest unit tests'

steps.title: Semantic Commit Generation
steps.description: Automate the Git commit process to ensure your project history is readable, professional, and audit-ready for your clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the last changes and generate a semantic commit message following Conventional Commits format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'review changes and generate conventional commit message'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Automate the deployment process so every merge to main triggers a build and test sequence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and Vercel deployment on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create github actions workflow for ci/cd'

pro_tips: Always use a 'Context File' (.context.md) in your root to keep the AI aligned with your specific stack preferences during long sessions.
Force the AI to write unit tests before implementation; it reduces debugging time by 40% in complex SaaS logic.
Use Antigravity to generate documentation as you code, keeping your README.md synced with your actual API endpoints.
Configure Claude Code to ignore node_modules globally to keep the token context window clean and efficient.
Perform a 'Git Squash' on your local feature branch before merging to main to keep the client-facing repo history clean and professional.
content.next_steps: Now that your environment is automated, focus on building your first micro-SaaS module and deploying it to Vercel.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the boilerplate and start building the future of your freelance business with AI precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
